name: Auth0 Development
goal: Pass the Auth0 Certified Developer exam (Okta certification). The exam has two parts: Part I is DOMC (Discrete Option Multiple Choice) covering planning, authentication, user management, customization, security, monitoring, and the Management API. Part II is hands-on practical tasks (~50% of score) performed in a live Auth0 tenant — SSO setup, passwordless, custom DB connections, social connections, bulk migration, Organizations + RBAC, token enrichment via Actions, Universal Login branding, custom Actions, and MFA.

concepts.name: Identity and Access Management Fundamentals

history.notes: Taught (finally) the three previously-untaught items flagged in the 05-29 regrade, then assessed ONLY those. (a) Protocol-layer normalization mechanics: SAML assertion -> verify XML-DSig -> trust cert sourced from SAML metadata (the JWKS analogue, refreshed on cert rotation) -> attribute mapping (arbitrary URI attr names -> standard OIDC claims email/name) -> re-mint fresh JWS/RS256 OIDC ID token. Mark NAILED the trust-boundary chain: app verifies Auth0's JWKS not Okta's cert because Auth0 absorbs upstream trust and re-signs. (b) Opaque vs JWT access tokens end-to-end: no audience param at /authorize -> opaque reference token, only valid at /userinfo (passed as Authorization: Bearer, NO scope query param, claims returned are fixed by the scopes GRANTED AT ISSUANCE); audience param -> self-contained JWT verifiable locally via JWKS. Mark retained Bearer-header + no-scope-param from prior concept; LEARNED the 'granted-at-issuance scopes determine /userinfo claims' piece (was 'not sure'). (c) ID-token-to-API bug: aud=client_id is the give-away, wrong-audience -> 401. EARLY ERROR-CODE SLIP corrected: said '404 forbidden' -> taught 401=invalid/wrong-aud, 403=forbidden (post-auth, lacks permission), 404=not found. SECOND correction: initially framed the 'API accepts client_id as aud' hole as 'access without authenticating' -> corrected to audience-binding broken => token substitution + loss of authZ data (ID token carries no scope/permissions). ASSESSMENT: Q1 (dual-aud /reports API, name the attack + fix): 0.8 -- correct that any client_id token now passes + correct fix (aud=API identifier only), but walked attack steps thinly and could NOT name the type; taught = token substitution / confused deputy / audience confusion, concrete walk = low-priv dashboard user replays their dashboard token at the privileged API. Q2 (SAML new-hire misconception, teach-back): 0.9 -- app verifies Auth0's re-minted JWS not SAML; named verify-XML-DSig + re-mint as the two concrete steps; omitted attribute-mapping + metadata; minor framing nit ('re-mint the signature' -> re-mint a token). Q3 INTERLEAVED (jwt_anatomy validation order + opaque contrast): 1.0 -- full ordered validation (decode header -> alg+kid -> JWKS public key -> verify sig over header.payload -> THEN iss/exp/aud) and correct that Login A opaque token can't be base64url-decoded at all. Interleaved jwt_anatomy item CORRECT -> no decay signal. NEXT REVIEW focus: (a) explicitly NAME 'token substitution / confused deputy / audience confusion' when audience binding breaks; (b) drill the concrete attacker walk-through for dual-aud misconfig (low-priv user -> token replay at privileged API); (c) all three taught items retained well -- raise difficulty / push transfer next time.

(c) audience-LOCATION (06-13 Part II focus): given a fetch() to the billing API w/ ?audience=... query param -- correctly said OMIT audience on the API call, but MISSED the Authorization: Bearer header (guessed client_id/secret). Corrected: resource API NEVER sees client_id/secret (those went to Auth0); its only input is the Bearer token; audience moves INSIDE the token as the aud claim, API compares aud to its own identifier. Lock-in CLEAN (minor actor slip: said 'the API sent the original request' -> it's the CLIENT app that requests the token). ASSESSMENT 2.1/3=0.70 FAIL (driven by Q1 iss regression). Fix = pin iss to known tenant / hardcode JWKS URI; signature alone does NOT stop forgery if verifier trusts the token to name its own key source. Overdue review (due 06-17, struggling m=0.35, 3 prior fails). RETRIEVAL-FIRST on 06-16 regression (issuer-confusion forgery, 0.9->0.3). MECHANISM ERROR caught+fixed: conflated issuer-confusion with ALGORITHM-confusion -- claimed attacker signs with 'HS256 private key' and 'deceptively keeps alg:RS256'. Re-taught: this exploit is PURE RS256, attacker uses their OWN RSA keypair + OWN JWKS at OWN iss, signature genuinely valid (wrong signer); alg field is honest, deception is ALL in iss. Algorithm-confusion (RS256->HS256, use public key as HMAC secret) is a SEPARATE attack that THIS code blocks via algorithms:['RS256']. DISCRIMINATOR check CLEAN: 'pin governs HOW sig is checked / which alg; issuer-confusion is WHICH KEY -- pin satisfied by attacker's real RS256 sig'. FIX written correctly (hardcode jwksUri to tenant constant, NOT from token; add issuer+audience to jwt.verify); sharpened issuer=full tenant URL w/ trailing slash not literal 'Auth0'; recalled aud=API identifier UNPROMPTED.
